title: oxycoccus palustris var. intermedius
intro: Oxycoccus palustris var. intermedius, also known as a type of cranberry, is a low-growing shrub native to bogs and marshy areas in Northern Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections due to its proanthocyanidin content, which helps prevent bacterial adhesion to the bladder and urinary tract walls. The health benefits of Oxycoccus palustris var. intermedius include reducing the risk of kidney stones, preventing the formation of bacteria that cause UTIs, and supporting the immune system. Oxycoccus palustris var. intermedius is often consumed as a tea, capsule, or dried fruit, and is also used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
